generated_description: Adversaries may modify an existing Windows service's configuration, specifically its ImagePath registry value, to execute arbitrary commands or scripts. In this observed activity, a PowerShell command was configured to run under the context of a legitimate service, indicating an attempt to establish persistence or achieve privilege escalation. The service's ImagePath was subsequently restored to its original value, potentially as a cleanup action or part of a testing sequence.

generated_description: Adversaries modified an existing Windows service's configuration, specifically its image path, to execute a PowerShell command. This action was performed using the `sc.exe` utility and subsequently started the service to achieve execution of the malicious command. Following this, the adversary accessed the memory of the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) process, indicating an attempt to dump credentials. The activity was conducted at a high integrity level under a non-system user account, and the service configuration was later reverted.
